title: float/double + IEEE + isnan/isinf/isfinite
manager: IEEE-754 floating point for the cases that genuinely want it — physics, graphics, approximate measurement — with the sharp edges made first-class rather than hidden. `isnan`, `isinf`, and `isfinite` are language builtins, because in FSL a NaN sneaking into a guard should be a question you can explicitly ask, not a silent surprise that makes a transition fire wrong. Float is explicit, so nobody inherits binary drift by accident when they wanted exact.
cs: float (32) / double (64) IEEE-754; isnan/isinf/isfinite predicates; no silent promotion from int (W6.7); comparisons total per W6.33.
